title: Update README TUI Controls And Interaction Notes
description: Revise the README TUI section so the documented controls and behavior reflect panel focus switching, detail scrolling commands, and mouse interactions introduced by the runtime changes.
acceptance_criteria: README key bindings document `Tab` and `Shift+Tab` focus switching.
README explains when `j/k`, arrows, paging keys, and home/end affect detail scrolling versus list selection.
README documents required mouse click and wheel behavior for list and detail panels.
Documentation remains consistent with the implemented runtime behavior and does not imply a layout redesign.

handoff_summary.completed: Revised the `Interactive TUI` documentation in README to clarify that `Tab` and `Shift+Tab` switch focus between list and detail panels; `j/k`, arrow keys, `PageUp/PageDown`, and `Home/End` act on the currently focused panel; the focused panel keeps the accent border; selecting a new bead resets detail scroll; clicking a list row focuses and selects it; clicking the detail panel only moves focus; and mouse wheel behavior is routed per hovered panel.
handoff_summary.remaining: No additional documentation work is required for this bead unless B0050 changes the shipped TUI interactions again.
handoff_summary.risks: README.md is a shared documentation surface, so concurrent edits to the TUI section could cause a merge conflict. The wording here is aligned to the current `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` behavior in this worktree.
handoff_summary.next_action: Have the review agent verify the README wording against the final B0050 runtime state during feature review.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Potential text-level conflicts with any sibling bead also editing the README TUI section or nearby command documentation.

conflict_risks: Potential text-level conflicts with any sibling bead also editing the README TUI section or nearby command documentation.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise documentation changes and remaining gaps.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
